work_items.description:
Add full parent hierarchy to breadcrumbs. Currently shows 'Home &gt; Project &gt; TASK-123', should show 'Home &gt; Project &gt; Epic &gt; Story &gt; Task'. All data already cached in AppState, just need to walk parent chain using ParentId. Zero performance impact - O(depth) where depth = 3-4 max. Frontend-only change in WorkItemDetail.razor.

## Backend Protection (IMPORTANT)

**Circular references are IMPOSSIBLE in production data:**
- Backend validates parent changes in `backend/crates/pm-ws/src/handlers/work_item.rs:586-634`
- `check_circular_reference` function walks parent chain before accepting parent updates
- MAX_DEPTH = 100 on backend prevents excessively deep hierarchies
- Any attempt to create A→B→C→A cycle is REJECTED server-side with validation error

**Frontend Implementation:**
- NO cycle detection needed (backend guarantees data integrity)
- Simple MAX_DEPTH = 20 limit for defensive programming only
- Handles edge cases: missing parents, race conditions, corrupted state
- Uses ILogger for warnings, not Console.WriteLine

## Implementation Details

**Stories:**
1. **PONE-57**: Parent chain traversal logic (BuildParentChain method with depth limit)
2. **PONE-58**: Breadcrumb rendering HTML (loop through parents, show keys with title tooltips)
3. **PONE-59**: Edge cases and tests (depth limit, missing parents, integration tests)

**Breadcrumb Display:**
- Shows work item keys (PONE-57) instead of full titles for compact display
- Hover tooltips show full titles (title="{work item title}" attribute)
- Example: Home / P1 / PONE-56 / PONE-57 / PONE-60
- All parent links clickable, current item non-clickable with class="current"

**Performance:**
- O(depth) where depth ≤ 4 typical, ≤ 20 enforced by frontend limit
- All lookups from in-memory AppState cache
- Zero DB queries, zero WebSocket calls
- Breadcrumb updates dynamically on navigation

let state = AppState {

work_items.description:
## Context

The epic targets <100ms total render time for drag-drop to Done column (down from multi-second stalls). This task validates the improvement with actual measurements and ensures cross-browser compatibility after replacing Radzen with HTML5 native drag-drop.

## Performance Measurement

Use Chrome DevTools Performance tab to record before/after render times:

1. **Baseline** (before any changes): Record a drag from Review → Done with ~108 items in Done column. Note total render time.
2. **After fix**: Same operation after all PONE-190 tasks are implemented. Target: <100ms total render time.
3. **Record both measurements** as a comment on PONE-190 for posterity.

## Specific Test Scenarios

All must pass before the epic can be marked done:

- [ ] **Drag card Review → Done** (100+ items in Done) — confirm fast, no visible stall
- [ ] **Drag card Done → ToDo** (1 item in ToDo) — confirm fast
- [ ] **Scroll Done column** (100+ items) — confirm smooth virtualized scrolling, NO scroll jitter, no content clipping on Epics/Stories/Tasks
- [ ] **Epic cards** show full child progress bars (not truncated)
- [ ] **Story cards** show full metadata (not truncated)
- [ ] **Rapid successive drags** (drag 3 cards quickly) — confirm no echo re-renders, no stale state
- [ ] **BlockedIndicator** updates correctly when dependencies change, without subscription spam in console
- [ ] **Column drag-over highlight** appears on dragenter, disappears on dragleave/drop
- [ ] **Drag to invalid drop target** (outside columns) — no crash, no state corruption

## Browser Compatibility

HTML5 drag-and-drop replaces Radzen's abstraction layer. Verify:

- [ ] **Chrome/Edge** (Chromium): Full functionality (primary target)
- [ ] **Firefox**: HTML5 drag-drop works (may need `event.dataTransfer` for some edge cases)
- [ ] **Safari**: Known quirk — `dragstart` requires `event.dataTransfer.setData()` or the drag silently fails. Verify our implementation handles this.

## Known Limitations to Document

- [ ] **Mobile/touch**: HTML5 drag events do NOT fire on touch devices. Document as known limitation. If mobile Kanban drag-drop is needed, a touch polyfill (e.g., `mobile-drag-drop`) or separate touch handler would be required as a follow-up.
- [ ] **Keyboard drag-drop**: HTML5 drag events only fire for mouse/pointer interactions, NOT keyboard. Radzen's `RadzenDropZoneContainer` provided built-in keyboard drag-drop support (evidenced by `.keyboard-drag-overlay` CSS removed in PONE-201). This is a known WCAG 2.1 Level A regression. Verify that the follow-up keyboard accessibility task exists under PONE-194 and is properly scoped (Space to grab, Arrow keys to move between columns, Space to drop, Esc to cancel).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Performance measurement recorded (before/after) as comment on PONE-190
- All test scenarios above pass
- Browser compatibility verified on Chrome + at least one of Firefox/Safari
- Known limitations (mobile/touch AND keyboard accessibility) documented as follow-up tasks
- Keyboard accessibility follow-up task exists under PONE-194

## File

N/A — this is a verification/testing task, no code changes
work_items.title: Measure drag-drop render performance and verify cross-browser

## Problem

The pm-cli `work-item update` command has no `--position` flag. The position field exists in the database (`pm_work_items.position` INTEGER) and is returned in API responses, but cannot be set via the CLI.

This forced a direct SQLite hack during PONE-39 audit to reorder tasks by implementation priority.

## Scope

Add `--position &lt;INT&gt;` as an optional parameter to `pm work-item update`. Should follow the same patterns as `--story-points` (optional integer, passed through to the update request).

## Likely touchpoints
- `pm-cli` argument parsing (clap struct for work-item update)
- `UpdateWorkItemRequest` protobuf message (may need a new field)
- Backend handler for work item updates (pm-ws or pm-api)
- Position validation (non-negative integer)

## Out of scope (for now)
- Drag-and-drop reordering (bulk position updates)
- Auto-gapping positions (e.g., positions 10, 20, 30 for easy insertion)
- Position constraints (uniqueness within parent)
work_items.title: Add --position flag to pm-cli work-item update

work_items.description:
## Context

PONE-199 replaces Radzen's `RadzenDropZoneContainer` with HTML5 native drag-drop + Blazor `<Virtualize>`. HTML5 drag events only fire for mouse/pointer interactions — they do NOT support keyboard-initiated drags. Radzen's drop zone provided built-in keyboard drag-drop support (evidenced by `.keyboard-drag-overlay` CSS that was removed in PONE-201).

This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A regression that must be addressed as a follow-up to the performance optimization epic.

## Requirements

Implement keyboard handlers on KanbanBoard.razor so that users can move cards between columns without a mouse:

1. **Focus management**: Cards must be focusable (`tabindex="0"`) and visually indicate focus state
2. **Grab**: Press Space or Enter on a focused card to "grab" it (enter keyboard-drag mode)
3. **Move**: Arrow Left/Right to select target column while in keyboard-drag mode
4. **Drop**: Press Space or Enter to drop the card into the selected column
5. **Cancel**: Press Escape to cancel the keyboard drag and return the card to its original position
6. **Announcements**: Update `_announcement` (ARIA live region) at each step so screen readers narrate what's happening

## Implementation Notes

- Add `@onkeydown` handler to each card's `<div>` wrapper
- Track keyboard-drag state separately from mouse drag (`_keyboardDragItemId`, `_keyboardTargetColumn`)
- Show a visual indicator on the target column (reuse `.drag-over` CSS class)
- The actual status update reuses the existing `HandleDrop()` method
- Consider a `KeyboardDragState` enum: `None`, `Grabbed`, `SelectingColumn`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tab to a card, press Space → card is "grabbed", screen reader announces "Grabbed [title]"
- [ ] Arrow Left/Right → target column highlights, screen reader announces column name
- [ ] Space to drop → card moves, screen reader announces "Moved [title] to [column]"
- [ ] Escape → cancel, screen reader announces "Drag cancelled"
- [ ] Full keyboard-only workflow works without any mouse interaction
- [ ] `just build-frontend` compiles
- [ ] `just test-frontend` passes

## File

`frontend/ProjectManagement.Components/WorkItems/KanbanBoard.razor`
work_items.title: Implement keyboard drag-drop for WCAG 2.1 compliance

work_items.description:
Adding the `validation` field to `HandlerContext` and `AppState` will break every test fixture that constructs them. There are 10 `HandlerContext::new` calls and 4 `AppState` constructions in test code that all need updating.

The fix is the same everywhere: add `ValidationConfig::default()` as the new parameter/field.

## HandlerContext::new calls

Add `ValidationConfig::default()` as the last argument to every call.

All in `backend/crates/pm-ws/tests/`:
- `comment_handler_tests.rs:138` (create_context)
- `comment_handler_tests.rs:150` (create_context_as)
- `sprint_handler_tests.rs:103` (create_context)
- `time_entry_handler_tests.rs:139` (create_context)
- `time_entry_handler_tests.rs:151` (create_context_as)
- `dependency_handler_tests.rs:144` (create_context)
- `activity_log_handler_tests.rs:98` (ctx)
- `llm_context_handler_tests.rs:39` (ctx)
- `dispatcher_integration.rs:119` (create_context)
- `subscription_handler_tests.rs:17`

Before (same pattern at all 10 sites):

```rust
HandlerContext::new(
    msg_id.to_string(),
    self.user_id,
    self.pool.clone(),
    self.circuit_breaker.clone(),
    &quot;test-connection&quot;.to_string(),
    registry,
)
```

After:

```rust
use pm_config::ValidationConfig;

HandlerContext::new(
    msg_id.to_string(),
    self.user_id,
    self.pool.clone(),
    self.circuit_breaker.clone(),
    &quot;test-connection&quot;.to_string(),
    registry,
    ValidationConfig::default(),
)
```

## AppState constructions

Add `validation: ValidationConfig::default()` field to every struct literal.

Locations:
- `backend/crates/pm-ws/tests/common/test_server.rs:143`
- `backend/pm-server/tests/common/mod.rs:37`
- `backend/pm-server/src/tests/api/extractors/user_id.rs:27`
- `backend/pm-server/src/tests/api/extractors/user_id.rs:111`

Before (same pattern at all 4 sites):

```rust
AppState {
    pool,
    circuit_breaker,
    jwt_validator: None,
    desktop_user_id: &quot;test-user&quot;.to_string(),
    rate_limiter_factory: RateLimiterFactory::default(),
    registry,
    metrics: Metrics::new(),
    shutdown,
    config: ConnectionConfig::default(),
    api_config: ApiConfig::default(),
}
```

After:

```rust
use pm_config::ValidationConfig;

AppState {
    pool,
    circuit_breaker,
    jwt_validator: None,
    desktop_user_id: &quot;test-user&quot;.to_string(),
    rate_limiter_factory: RateLimiterFactory::default(),
    registry,
    metrics: Metrics::new(),
    shutdown,
    config: ConnectionConfig::default(),
    api_config: ApiConfig::default(),
    validation: ValidationConfig::default(),
}
```

This is a mechanical change but critical — without it, no tests compile.

Verify: `just test-backend` (all tests must compile and pass)

**Dependencies**: Must run after ALL other tasks — this fixes test compilation broken by PONE-109 and PONE-111. Last task in the epic.
work_items.title: Update all test fixtures: add ValidationConfig to HandlerContext and AppState constructors

## PONE-54: Add syntax highlighting for code blocks

**Updated during audit (2026-02-09):** Changed from Highlight.js CDN (JavaScript) to Markdown.ColorCode (pure .NET) to maintain zero-JS constraint.

### Implementation

**Package used:** `Markdown.ColorCode` (already installed in PONE-40)

This is a Markdig extension that integrates ColorCode for automatic syntax highlighting.

### Changes Made

**MarkdownRenderer.razor:**
- Added `@using Markdown.ColorCode` directive
- Added `.UseColorCode()` to Markdig pipeline
- Preserves `.DisableHtml()` for XSS protection

**MarkdownRenderer.razor.css:**
- Code block styling (background, border, padding)
- Monospace font family
- Inline code styling
- Syntax highlighting colors (keyword, string, comment, className, typeName, number, method, preprocessorKeyword)

### Supported Languages

ColorCode automatically detects language from fenced code blocks:
- C# (`csharp`, `cs`)
- JavaScript (`javascript`, `js`)
- TypeScript (`typescript`, `ts`)
- Python (`python`, `py`)
- SQL, Rust, HTML, CSS, XML, and more

`just build-cs-components && just build-cs-wasm`

work_items.description:
## Summary

The `MessageValidator` in `pm-ws` hardcodes all field length limits (comment=5000, title=200, description=10000, sprint name=100). Meanwhile, `ValidationConfig` in `pm-config` already defines `max_title_length` and `max_description_length` as configurable fields — but they are **never wired to the validator**. The validator ignores them entirely and uses its own hardcoded values.

## Goals

1. Add `max_comment_length` and `max_sprint_name_length` as new configurable fields in `ValidationConfig`
2. Wire `ValidationConfig` through the handler pipeline so `MessageValidator` uses config values instead of hardcoded literals
3. Fix the existing disconnect where `max_title_length` and `max_description_length` exist in config but are never consumed

## Scope

**Making configurable** (content/text limits users may want to adjust):
- Comment content: 5,000 chars (new field)
- Sprint name: 100 chars (new field)
- Work item title: 200 chars (already in config — wire it up)
- Work item description: 10,000 chars (already in config — wire it up)

**Keeping hardcoded** (structural/format constraints, not content limits):
- Project key: 2-20 chars (format constraint with alphanumeric rules)
- Pagination: max 1,000 (operational limit)
- UUID/subscription fields (protocol constraints)

## Key Files

- `backend/crates/pm-config/src/validation_config.rs` — Config struct + defaults + bounds
- `backend/crates/pm-config/src/config.rs` — Env overrides + logging
- `backend/crates/pm-ws/src/message_validator.rs` — All validation logic (currently hardcoded)
- `backend/crates/pm-ws/src/handlers/context.rs` — HandlerContext (needs ValidationConfig)
- `backend/crates/pm-ws/src/app_state.rs` — AppState (needs ValidationConfig)
- `backend/crates/pm-ws/src/web_socket_connection.rs` — WebSocketConnection (threads config)
- `backend/crates/pm-ws/src/handlers/{comment,work_item,sprint,project}.rs` — All callers
- `backend/pm-server/src/api/{comments,work_items}/` — REST API callers
- `backend/config.example.toml` — User-facing config docs
work_items.title: Make Validation Limits Configurable via pm-config

work_items.description:
The `sanitize_string()` function in `pm-ws` HTML-encodes user input (replacing `&lt;`, `&gt;`, `&`, `"`, `'` with HTML entities) before storing it in SQLite. This corrupts data — especially work item descriptions containing code snippets — and provides zero security benefit in our stack.

**Why this encoding is unnecessary:**
- Blazor auto-escapes `@variable` output by default (XSS-safe without server-side encoding)
- Data is transported as JSON (REST) and Protobuf (WebSocket), never embedded in raw HTML
- SQLx uses parameterized queries (no SQL injection risk from string content)
- The encoding is applied at the storage layer, permanently destroying the original text

**Why it actively causes harm:**
- Code snippets with `"`, `&lt;`, `&gt;`, `&` are mangled (e.g., `&mut` becomes `&amp;mut`)
- The function has a replacement-order bug: `&` is replaced THIRD, so it double-encodes the `&` from earlier `&lt;`/`&gt;` replacements
- Data that passes through sanitize_string() more than once gets progressively more corrupted
- PONE-106's description has 8 instances of `&amp;quot;` and 2 of `&amp;amp;` in the database

**Scope:** Remove all HTML encoding from `sanitize_string()` (keep trim), update tests, and clean up existing corrupted data in the database.
work_items.title: Remove HTML Encoding from sanitize_string

work_items.description:
Enable all REST API endpoints and CLI commands to accept human-readable display keys (e.g. PONE-126) and project keys (e.g. PONE) in addition to UUIDs.

**Problem**: LLMs using the /pm CLI skill must currently discover UUIDs through multi-step queries (list projects → scan for project UUID → list work items → scan for matching display_key → extract UUID). This is slow, error-prone, and wastes tokens.

**Solution**: Server-side resolution. Since the CLI passes raw strings directly into URL paths, making the server smart about accepting both UUIDs and display keys means zero CLI code changes. All clients (CLI, LLMs, curl, future integrations) benefit automatically.

**Approach**:
- Add a shared `resolve.rs` module in pm-server with `parse_display_key`, `resolve_project`, and `resolve_work_item` helpers
- Update all REST handlers that accept work item IDs or project IDs in URL paths/query params
- Update CLI help text and SKILL.md documentation

**Existing code to reuse**:
- `ProjectRepository::find_by_key()` at `pm-db/src/repositories/project_repository.rs:148`
- `WorkItemRepository::find_by_project_and_number()` at `pm-db/src/repositories/work_item_repository.rs:516`
- Composite unique index on `(project_id, item_number)` ensures fast lookups

**Scope exclusion**: Request body fields (create_work_item parent_id, create_time_entry work_item_id, etc.) are deferred since LLMs typically chain command output which already includes UUIDs.
work_items.title: Display Key Resolution for REST API & CLI

work_items.description:
Verify that the new trim-only sanitize_string implementation correctly handles all edge cases including whitespace, special characters, and code snippets.

**Implementation:**

```rust
pub fn sanitize_string(s: &str) -> String {
    s.trim().to_string()
}
```

**Test cases to verify:**
- Empty strings return empty
- Whitespace-only strings return empty
- Leading/trailing whitespace is removed
- Special characters like `<`, `>`, `&`, `"`, `'` pass through unchanged
- Code snippets with operators are preserved

This validates the fix from PONE-126 is working correctly.
work_items.title: Test sanitize_string function behavior

work_items.description:
The Kanban board has significant lag when dropping cards. Root cause: `ViewModelFactory.CreateMany` is O(N²) — for each of ~131 items, it scans all items to find children/grandchildren for progress bars. This fires 3 times per drop (optimistic, confirmed, own broadcast).

**Fix:** Embed pre-computed `ancestor_ids` and `descendant_ids` in the WorkItem proto message. The backend computes hierarchy once; the frontend uses it for O(N) progress lookups via dictionary instead of scanning.

**Performance impact:**
- Backend: One extra `find_by_project` query per create/update (~131 rows, sub-ms on SQLite)
- Frontend: `CreateMany` drops from O(N²) to O(N). For 131 items: ~17,000 comparisons → ~131 dictionary lookups, repeated 3x per drop

**Implementation:** Follow the stories and tasks in this epic on the agile board.
work_items.title: Embed hierarchy in WorkItem proto to eliminate O(N²) board lag

work_items.description:
## Problem

Moving a work item to the "Done" column (~108 items) is extremely slow, while moving to "ToDo" (1 item) is fast. The root cause is a **render cascade** where a single drag-drop triggers up to **6 full re-renders** of the entire board, each rebuilding all 165 ViewModels and re-rendering all BlockedIndicator components.

## Root Causes (in order of impact)

1. **Double `StateHasChanged` per event** — `HandleStateChanged()` calls `RefreshData()` (which calls `ApplyFilters()` → `StateHasChanged()`), then also calls `InvokeAsync(StateHasChanged)`. Two renders per event.
2. **3 separate events per drag** — Optimistic update, server response, and broadcast echo each fire `NotifyChanged()` → `AppState.OnStateChanged` → full re-render cascade. That's 6 render passes total.
3. **`ViewModelFactory.CreateMany` rebuilds ALL 165 ViewModels** on every render — no memoization, includes ChildProgress computation for every epic/story.
4. **108 `BlockedIndicator`s** each independently subscribe to `DependencyStore.OnChanged` and call their own `StateHasChanged` — 108 independent re-render requests per dependency store change.
5. **No virtualization** — All cards rendered even when off-screen.

## Expected Impact

| Fix | Before | After | Reduction |
|-----|--------|-------|-----------|
| Double StateHasChanged | 2 renders/event | 1 render/event | 50% |
| Broadcast echo | 3 events/drag | 2 events/drag | 33% |
| Virtualization | 108 cards rendered | ~10 cards rendered | 90% |
| BlockedIndicator | 108 subscriptions | 0 subscriptions | 100% |
| **Combined** | **6 × 108 = 648 component renders** | **2 × 10 = 20 component renders** | **97%** |

## Files Changed

- `frontend/ProjectManagement.Components/WorkItems/KanbanBoard.razor`
- `frontend/ProjectManagement.Services/State/WorkItemStore.cs`
- `frontend/ProjectManagement.Components/Dependencies/BlockedIndicator.razor`
- `frontend/ProjectManagement.Components/WorkItems/KanbanColumn.razor` (deleted — inlined into KanbanBoard)
- `frontend/ProjectManagement.Components/wwwroot/css/kanban.css`

## Verification

1. `just build-frontend` — compiles
2. `just test-frontend` — existing tests pass
3. Manual: drag card from Review → Done — confirm it's fast
4. Manual: drag card from Done → ToDo — confirm it's fast
5. Manual: scroll Done column (100+ items) — confirm smooth virtualized scrolling with no scroll jitter
6. Manual: drag card — confirm column highlights on dragover
7. **Performance measurement**: Use browser DevTools Performance tab to record before/after render times for a drag-drop to Done column. Target: <100ms total render time (down from current multi-second stalls)
work_items.title: Kanban Board Drag-Drop Performance Optimization
Remove Independent BlockedIndicator Subscriptions

work_items.description:
## Problem

The Done column contains ~108 cards, but the visible viewport only shows ~8-10 at a time. Currently, **all 108 cards are rendered** on every re-render — including their KanbanCard components, BlockedIndicators, child progress bars, and badge styles. This is the single largest contributor to per-render cost.

The current implementation uses RadzenDropZoneContainer + RadzenDropZone which does not support virtualization and renders all items in every zone.

## Solution

Replace the Radzen drag-drop system with HTML5 native drag-drop + Blazor's built-in <Virtualize> component. This renders only the cards visible in each column's scroll viewport (~8-10 per column).

## Key Design Decisions

- HTML5 drag events (@ondragstart, @ondragover:preventDefault, @ondrop) replace Radzen's RadzenDropZoneContainer
- <Virtualize> with ItemSize=120 (approximate card height in px) replaces full item rendering
- KanbanColumn.razor is deleted — its logic (header, badge, empty state) is inlined into KanbanBoard since it was just a thin wrapper around RadzenDropZone
- Column highlight on dragover uses a _dragOverColumn state field + CSS class .drag-over

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Done column with 100+ items renders only visible cards
- [ ] Drag-drop between columns still works (HTML5 native)
- [ ] Column visually highlights when a card is dragged over it
- [ ] Column headers show correct item counts and badge colors
- [ ] Empty columns show the empty-state message
- [ ] Scroll within a column works smoothly
- [ ] just build-frontend compiles
- [ ] just test-frontend passes
work_items.title: Virtualize Kanban Column Card Rendering

work_items.description:
## Overview

Two friction points when LLMs use the pm CLI:

1. **Reading output** — LLMs pipe JSON to `/tmp/*.json` and must request shell write permissions each time. Noisy and breaks the permission workflow.
2. **Writing descriptions** — Markdown descriptions with backticks and code blocks cannot be passed cleanly as `--description` CLI args. LLMs fall back to `cat >>` hacks that require extra permissions.

## Solution

TOML fixes both:
- Triple-quoted multiline strings handle markdown natively — backticks, code blocks, and special characters require no escaping.
- `--output-toml <PATH>` writes any command response directly to a TOML file — no shell piping needed.
- `--from-toml <PATH>` on `work-item create` and `work-item update` lets LLMs write a structured file and pass it in one command.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `$PM <any-command> --output-toml /path/file.toml` writes the response as valid TOML; stdout still receives JSON.
- `$PM work-item create --from-toml ./item.toml` creates a work item from a TOML file; CLI flags override file values.
- `$PM work-item update <id> --version N --from-toml ./update.toml` updates from a TOML file; CLI flags override.
- Null fields (assignee_id, sprint_id, etc.) are stripped before TOML serialization.
- SKILL.md is updated with documented flags and LLM workflow examples.
work_items.title: TOML I/O for pm CLI — LLM-Friendly File Interface
